title: Part II: Understanding What You Just Brewed

contents.text: Every ingredient used in alchemy carries [color=#EC8FFF]Essences[/color] - invisible properties that determine what effects it can contribute to a brew. Each essence is tied to a specific effect (such as Speed or Regeneration) and has a weight that defines how strongly it manifests. At first, these properties are unknown, and uncovering them is the foundation of all alchemical work.

Alchemy is built on observation. Different tools and actions reveal different parts of an ingredient, and these methods [color=#EC8FFF]do not replace each other[/color] - each uncovers a specific layer of information, gradually forming a complete understanding.

The [color=#EC8FFF]Alchemist's Eye[/color] is worn as a helmet and allows you to inspect ingredients directly in their tooltip. It does not reveal new information on its own, but instead displays everything you have already discovered - including known [color=#EC8FFF]Essences[/color] and revealed [color=#EC8FFF]Traits[/color] - as a live, readable summary.

[color=#EC8FFF]Solvents[/color] are used to uncover the hidden essences within an ingredient. Using one in a Brewing Stand with an ingredient produces an [color=#EC8FFF]Extract[/color] containing a single random essence. Picking up the extract permanently reveals that essence, and repeated use gradually uncovers the full composition. Once all essences are known, the ingredient's [color=#EC8FFF]Aspect[/color] becomes visible.

Using an ingredient in a cauldron reveals how it behaves in practice. Each ingredient has different [color=#EC8FFF]Traits[/color] depending on the role it takes - [color=#8fe1ff]Base, Catalyst, or Inhibitor[/color]. When an ingredient is used in a specific slot for the first time, its trait for that role becomes permanently known, meaning that full understanding requires experimenting with it in [color=#EC8FFF]all three roles[/color].

Every fully analyzed ingredient belongs to an [color=#EC8FFF]Aspect[/color] - a category reflecting its nature. Aspects are only revealed once all essences of an ingredient have been discovered and play an important role in advanced brewing.
